sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Team Standings
sections.text: Auxerre, currently sitting at 10th in the league with 41 points, host Nantes, who are positioned at 15th with 32 points, in an intriguing clash at the Stade de l'Abbe-Deschamps in Auxerre. This matchup sees a mid-table side looking to solidify their standing against a team fighting to climb away from the relegation zone. Both teams will be eager to secure vital points as the season progresses. The game is officiated by Marc Bollengier, known for averaging 3.5 yellow cards per game, which could influence the disciplinary tone of the match.

sections.subtitle: Historical Head-to-Head Insights
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between Auxerre and Nantes, the record is tight with Nantes holding a slight edge with 3 wins compared to Auxerre’s 2 wins, and no draws recorded. Their most recent encounter on 2024-08-25 saw Nantes triumph 2-0 at home, with Guirassy Bahereba earning Player of the Match honors with a Sofascore rating of 7.9. Interestingly, in head-to-head stats, Auxerre has failed to keep a clean sheet in 10 consecutive games against Nantes, while Nantes has been first to score in 5 of the last 7 meetings. Also, matches between these two often see less than 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 encounters, suggesting a potentially low-scoring affair.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Auxerre
sections.text: Auxerre boasts some standout performers, particularly Gaëtan Perrin, a midfielder who ranks 1st in the league for assists with 10 and 2nd for key passes (72) and big chances created (18). His average Sofascore rating of 7.34 and recent form over the last 5 matches (rating 7.36 with 1 goal and 3 assists) make him a critical asset. Hamed Junior Traorè, another midfielder, has overperformed his expected goals by 2.352, scoring 10 goals this season with a rating of 7.07. Up front, Sinayoko Lassine, with 4 goals and 7 assists, has also been in form recently, posting a 7.28 rating over the last 5 matches with 2 goals and 3 assists. Defender Jubal, with 6 goals and a solid 7.26 rating, adds aerial threat, especially given Auxerre’s 5th league rank for headers scored (8).

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Nantes
sections.text: For Nantes, Moses Simon stands out as a top performer, ranking 4th in the league for assists with 9 and 1st for successful dribbles per game at 2.1, holding a Sofascore rating of 7.08. Forward Matthis Abline has contributed 8 goals this season, overperforming his expected goals by 0.6028, and will be a key attacking threat with a rating of 6.9. Midfielder Douglas Augusto, with 4 goals and 2 assists, maintains a strong presence with a 7.05 rating. Defender Zeze Nathan, despite no goals or assists, has impressed defensively with a rating of 7.04, which could be vital against Auxerre’s attacking options.

sections.subtitle: Tactical and Statistical Trends - Auxerre
sections.text: Auxerre has shown a knack for late goals, with 25.5% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes and another 19.1% between the 61st and 75th minutes, totaling 20 goals in these late periods. They excel in converting big chances, ranking 1st in the league with a 50% conversion rate, and have missed the least big chances (32). Defensively, they rank 1st for interceptions per game at 10.28, 2nd for tackles per game at 19.56, and 3rd for clearances per game at 26.78. However, their ball possession is low at 42.16% (16th in the league), and they struggle with final third pressing, ranking 16th for possessions won in the final third per game at 3.44.

sections.subtitle: Tactical and Statistical Trends - Nantes
sections.text: Nantes also shows a tendency for late scoring, with 25.5% of their goals occurring between the 76th and 90th minutes, totaling 8 goals in this period. Their recent form shows no wins in the last 4 matches, which could weigh on their confidence. However, in head-to-head streaks, they often score first against Auxerre in 5 of the last 7 games. Their away team streak also indicates games with less than 10.5 corners in 5 of their last 7 matches, hinting at a potentially less open game in terms of set-piece opportunities.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Given referee Marc Bollengier’s average of 3.5 yellow cards per game and Auxerre’s season average of 1.97 yellow cards per game alongside their 12th league rank for least yellow cards (63 total), expect a moderately disciplined match. Head-to-head trends support this, with less than 4.5 cards in 9 of their last 10 meetings. In terms of corners, Auxerre averages 3.91 per game (16th in the league), and Nantes’ away streak shows less than 10.5 corners in 5 of 7 games, suggesting a game with limited corner action.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Standout Performers
sections.text: An interesting historical note is Fabien Centonze’s consistent performances against Nantes, starting in all 5 of the last matchups for Auxerre with an average Sofascore rating of 7.2, despite no goals or assists. Auxerre’s Assane Dioussé also stands out statistically, ranking 5th in the league for the percentage of long balls in total passes at 9.1%, showcasing their direct style at times. On the Nantes side, Moses Simon’s league-leading dribbling stats (2.1 successful dribbles per game) could exploit Auxerre’s 14th-ranked ground duels won percentage of 48.82%. Additionally, Auxerre ranks 3rd for fast breaks per game at 1.47, which could test Nantes’ defensive organization.
